feed.title: DaughterDrivenDev
feed.subtitle: Daughter Driven Development. Parents in Tech.

entry.title: Winter is here.
entry.content:
Winter is here.

Snow is piling up across Europe and other parts of the Northern Hemisphere. Slippery roads, canceled flights and trains. In the ideal movie version of parenthood at this time of year, parents build snowmen and go sledding down the perfect hill, scarves flapping in the wind.

Reality, however, looks a bit different.

The most anticipated information for us is posted on the display board at the kindergarten entrance. During this season, kids do not collect and trade Pokémon cards. They collect and trade illnesses.

A short summary from the board in our youngest child’s group during the last two weeks before Christmas: gastrointestinal infections (multiple times), hand, foot, and mouth disease (multiple times), scarlet fever (once), COVID (a lot).

And of course, the daily horror continues into the new year. Questions start popping up in our heads. How do you politely, without sounding like a terrible person, tell your child that maybe they should not hug and wrestle with Paul, whose entire T-shirt is covered in nasal secretions?

Other questions follow quickly.

How do you deal with the fallout from all of this? How do you throw away carefully planned schedules and time-boxed calendars? How do you tell your team that a deadline that once seemed very achievable might now be a stretch? Because yes, this small human needs us right now.

Every winter, we hear dozens of stories from parents. Some are painfully funny. Others create an instant sense of solidarity. And occasionally, you hear a genuinely helpful idea about how to cope with all of this.

So what is your story? How do you cope?

We are looking forward to hearing from you!

Marcin && Ola

entry.title: Hello fellow parent in tech,
entry.content:
Hello fellow parent in tech,

Thank you, you decided to become a part of our journey. In our first newsletter, we’d like to introduce ourselves quickly and explain how this project started.

So, how did we get here?

Marcin and I met in Dortmund somewhere around 2013. Both migrated as kids from Poland (names might have given that away, haven’t they) to small cities on the Westside of Germany. Both engineers already. We saw each other at meetups/conferences from time to time, but as a solo parent, my world was already so different from his. Our lines barely crossed. I moved to Berlin. Worked on open source projects and Firefox full-time. Spoke at and organized conferences. Enjoyed my life with the little one and lived a full daughter-driven-dev life.

Fast-forward to today…

I got a message from Marcin on LinkedIn, who now is a dad of 3 amazing kids. Totally random. He asked me if I wrote a book about how to survive as an engineer and a parent, as I lived that for such a long time already. Or, at least, if I could write one. As I said… That felt SO random. To be honest, I am an idiot most of the time. Of course, I misunderstood. I thought he asked me to write something like that TOGETHER. I actually liked that idea. He didn’t. Until he did…

Since then, we had so much fun talking about our lives, our ups and downs as parents and basically how to survive this wild ride. We know all of you experienced what we are talking about.

This is where YOU come in!

This is why we have set up this newsletter. It is not a one way street. We want to hear from you as well. We want you, your life, your experience to be a part of this project, because it gave us so much already to do this together.

If you find a minute in your busy life, maybe while hanging out on the playground or during your commute from kindergarten to work, it would be brilliant if you could share a bit about yourself. Maybe how many kids you have or what the biggest struggle is you are dealing with right now?

We are looking forward to hearing from you!

Marcin && Ola
